title: Tanker report - Week 3
summary:
Clean
The CPP tanker market has for the most part continued its drop this week. As with last week there are a couple of sectors that have managed to see small improvements. LR's in the Middle East Gulf have continued to freefall.

children.title: Clean
children.summary: The CPP tanker market has for the most part continued its drop this week. As with last week there are a couple of sectors that have managed to see small improvements.

children.title: VLCC
children.summary: The VLCC market rose marginally this week, except for the US Gulf to China route. 270,000mt Middle East Gulf to China recovered a modest two points to WS46.73, which translates into a round voyage TCE of $18,100 basis the Baltic Exchange's vessel description, while 280,000mt Middle East Gulf to US Gulf (via the cape/cape routing) is assessed half a point firmer than last Friday at WS35.

children.title: Suezmax
children.summary: The Suezmax market was a mixed-bag rate wise this week. The rate for 135,000mt CPC/Augusta remained flat at about the WS203-204 level (a round trip TCE of $115,100 per day).

children.title: Aframax
children.summary: In the North Sea market, rates for the 80,000mt Hound Point/Wilhelmshaven route rose a meagre one point to a WS162 (a round-trip daily TCE of $57,400). In the Mediterranean, the rate for 80,000mt Ceyhan/Lavera rose five points to WS244 (a daily round trip TCE of $92,300), with cargoes from Libya and the Black Sea able to tap into the Suezmax market, thereby capping the rates for the Aframax market.
